name: CFSC Checks Cashed Gramatan
description_short: CFSC Checks Cashed Gramatan is a community financial services location in Mt Vernon, NY offering check cashing, money orders, bill pay, and prepaid debit cards without requiring a bank account.
description_long:
CFSC Checks Cashed Gramatan operates as part of Community Financial Services, a multi-location check-cashing and alternative financial services network with branches throughout the New York area. The Mt Vernon location at 48 Gramatan Ave serves residents who need immediate access to cash and financial services outside traditional banking hours. The company specializes in serving unbanked and underbanked populations with no monthly fees or account minimums.

The Gramatan Ave location offers a comprehensive suite of services including check cashing (personal and business), money orders, electronic bill pay, domestic and international money transfers, prepaid debit cards, foreign currency exchange, prepaid cellular phone services, coin services, and tax refund services. They maintain extended hours six days a week (8:15 am–8:30 pm Mon-Fri, 8:15 am–6:30 pm Sat, 9:00 am–6:30 pm Sun) and provide bilingual service (Se Habla Espanol). The location also features ATM access for customers.

What distinguishes CFSC Gramatan is its focus on accessibility and convenience for consumers without bank accounts. Check cashing requires only a valid government-issued photo ID and basic personal information—no credit check, no waiting for fund clearance, and no banking relationship required. The prepaid debit card (Yes! Debit Account) also operates without a credit check. Their foreign currency exchange covers over 80 countries' currencies, and they offer business check cashing services. Extended hours and multiple nearby locations provide flexibility for working customers.

However, CFSC Gramatan is fundamentally a check-cashing service with no lending, credit-building, or financial education components. Customers should expect to pay check-cashing fees (amounts not disclosed on the website), and the company operates as a for-profit alternative financial services provider rather than a non-profit credit counselor. While convenient for immediate cash access, this is not a solution for customers seeking to build credit, consolidate debt, or access traditional banking products.
pros: No bank account required to cash checks—only valid photo ID and personal information needed
Extended hours: 8:15 am–8:30 pm weekdays, 8:15 am–6:30 pm Saturday, 9:00 am–6:30 pm Sunday
Bilingual services (Se Habla Espanol) available at this location
Prepaid debit card (Yes! Debit Account) available with no credit check required
Foreign currency exchange covering 80+ countries' currencies
Business check cashing services available on the spot
Multiple nearby CFSC locations provide fallback options (9+ locations listed in Bronx and Westchester)
cons: Check-cashing fees are not disclosed on the website, preventing transparent cost comparison
No credit-building, lending, or financial counseling services offered
Operating as a for-profit service, not a community bank or CDFI with member benefits
Limited to immediate cash access; does not address underlying financial stability or debt issues
best_for: Unbanked consumers who need immediate check cashing without a bank account
Immigrants and bilingual speakers sending international money transfers or exchanging foreign currency
Small business owners needing fast business check cashing without bank delays
Customers preferring alternative financial services with extended evening and weekend hours
diagnosis: CFSC Gramatan is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate check cashing and financial services outside traditional banking hours, particularly those who are bilingual or frequently transfer money internationally. The main caveat is that this is a convenience-based cash service, not a financial solution—customers seeking to build credit, address debt, or establish banking relationships should look elsewhere.
services: Personal check cashing (no bank account required)
